page_content: PLCY310 Nonproﬁt Leadership and Social Innovation in Action (3 Credits) Furthers students understanding of topics in leadership, social innovation, resource development, community mobilization through networks, and the role of policy making in creating change. This course will further students understanding of the creation and leadership of nonproﬁts, social ventures, governance and boards; strategic planning and partnerships; advocacy and public policy processes; community outreach; working in teams, effective communications, and cross-sector approaches to scaling up social impact. Prerequisite: PLCY 213.
